steps.title: Use destroyed evidence as the clue
steps.content: The sentence says much of the evidence of Sobekneferu's reign was destroyed. That means historians are unlikely to have a complete account of that period, even if they can still make limited or speculative claims.

steps.title: Rule out weaker meanings
steps.content: Choice A means shallow, which limited evidence would still allow. Choice B means creative, not factually complete. Choice C means doubtful, which destroyed evidence might make more likely rather than less likely. The remaining choice means thorough and complete. The answer is D.
